title: Same-sex marriage becomes legal in Colombia
description: Colombia's Constitutional Court cleared the way for same-sex couples to marry, with marriages beginning in 2016.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in three British territories
description: Same-sex marriage legislation passed and came into force in the Isle of Man, in Gibraltar, and in the British Antarctic Territory during 2016.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Five Mexican states enact same-sex marriage
description: Jalisco, Campeche, Colima, Michoacán and Morelos each adopted same-sex marriage measures that took effect during 2016, extending marriage equality across more of Mexico.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Greenland
description: Greenland's same-sex marriage law came into force in 2016, aligning the territory with the rest of the Kingdom of Denmark.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Faroe Islands approve same-sex marriage
description: The Faroese parliament voted in 2016 to legalize same-sex marriage in the Danish territory, ahead of the law later coming into force.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Guernsey and Ascension Island pass same-sex marriage laws
description: Legislatures in Guernsey and on Ascension Island approved same-sex marriage during 2016, ahead of the laws taking effect in those British territories.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Italy adopts civil unions for same-sex couples
description: Italy's parliament approved a civil-union law giving same-sex couples legal recognition, with the law coming into force the same year — the country's first nationwide recognition of same-sex partnerships.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Registered partnerships take effect in Aruba
description: Aruba, a constituent country of the Kingdom of the Netherlands, passed and implemented registered-partnership legislation covering same-sex couples in 2016.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Estonia's registered partnership law comes into force
description: Estonia's Registered Partnership Act, which covers same-sex couples, took effect in 2016, giving partnerships legal standing for the first time in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Portugal opens adoption to same-sex couples
description: Portugal enacted legislation in 2016 allowing same-sex couples to adopt children jointly, closing a gap left when marriage equality passed without adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Seychelles decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Seychelles repealed its colonial-era prohibition on consensual same-sex activity between men in 2016.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nauru decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Nauru removed criminal penalties for consensual same-sex conduct through a revision of its criminal code in 2016.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Belize strikes down its sodomy law
description: The Supreme Court of Belize ruled the criminal-code section punishing consensual same-sex intimacy unconstitutional, decriminalizing homosexuality in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Quebec and British Columbia add gender identity protections
description: The Canadian provinces of Quebec and British Columbia amended their human-rights legislation in 2016 to ban discrimination based on gender identity and gender expression.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Adidas adds an LGBT clause to athlete contracts
description: On 11 February 2016 Adidas announced that its athlete sponsorship contracts would include a clause ensuring a deal stays intact if the athlete comes out as LGBT.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Amelia Gapin appears on the cover of Women's Running
description: Marathoner Amelia Gapin became the first openly transgender woman featured on the cover of Women's Running magazine.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chris Mosier featured in ESPN's Body Issue and a Nike ad
description: Triathlete Chris Mosier became the first openly transgender athlete in ESPN The Magazine's Body Issue and also appeared in Nike's first advertisement with an openly transgender athlete.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Caitlyn Jenner on the cover of Sports Illustrated
description: Caitlyn Jenner became the first openly transgender person to appear on the cover of Sports Illustrated.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Amanda Nunes becomes the UFC's first openly gay champion
description: Brazilian fighter Amanda Nunes won a UFC title in 2016, making her the first openly gay champion in the organization's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Harrison Browne comes out as a transgender man
description: Harrison Browne of the National Women's Hockey League came out publicly, becoming the first openly transgender athlete in US professional team sports.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lea T takes part in the Rio Olympics opening ceremony
description: Model Lea T led the Brazilian delegation into the stadium by bicycle at the Rio 2016 opening ceremony, the first openly transgender person to take part in an Olympic opening ceremony.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Married couple Kate and Helen Richardson-Walsh win Olympic gold
description: Great Britain's women's field hockey team won gold at the Rio 2016 Games with Kate and Helen Richardson-Walsh on the roster, making them the first married same-sex couple to win Olympic medals.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly gay members of the Welsh Assembly take office
description: Hannah Blythyn, Jeremy Miles and Adam Price won seats in the 2016 Welsh Assembly election, becoming the assembly's first openly gay members.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Justine Greening comes out while serving in the British cabinet
description: UK government minister Justine Greening said publicly that she was in a same-sex relationship, making her the first openly LGB woman to serve as a British cabinet minister.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Anwen Muston elected to Wolverhampton City Council
description: Anwen Muston won a seat on Wolverhampton City Council, becoming the first openly transgender woman elected as a representative of the Labour Party.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Prince William appears on the cover of Attitude
description: Prince William became the first member of the British royal family on the cover of a gay magazine, appearing on the July 2016 issue of Attitude, where he also condemned the bullying of gay people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lord Ivar Mountbatten comes out as gay
description: Lord Ivar Mountbatten publicly came out and confirmed his relationship with airline cabin services director James Coyle, the first person in the extended British royal family to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Carl Austin-Behan sworn in as Manchester's Lord Mayor
description: Carl Austin-Behan took office as Lord Mayor of Manchester, the first openly gay person to hold the city's ceremonial post.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nicola Sturgeon addresses Glasgow Pride
description: Scottish First Minister Nicola Sturgeon spoke at Glasgow Pride 2017, the first serving UK prime minister or first minister to address a pride event, pledging continued work for equality across the UK. The source timeline files this under 2016, but the event took place in 2017.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Raffi Freedman-Gurspan named White House LGBT liaison
description: President Barack Obama appointed Raffi Freedman-Gurspan as the White House's primary liaison to the LGBT community, the first openly transgender person to hold the role.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maite Oronoz Rodríguez leads Puerto Rico's supreme court
description: Maite Oronoz Rodríguez became chief justice of Puerto Rico, the first openly gay person to serve as a chief justice anywhere in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Eric Fanning confirmed as Secretary of the Army
description: Eric Fanning became the first openly gay Secretary of the United States Army, the first openly gay civilian head of any branch of the US military.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Santa Clara County raises the transgender pride flag
description: Santa Clara County in California became the first county government in the United States to fly the transgender pride flag.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Trans United Fund is established
description: Transgender leaders launched Trans United Fund, the first 501(c)(4) advocacy organization of its kind focused on transgender political issues.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Misty Plowright wins a major-party US House primary
description: Misty Plowright became the first openly transgender candidate to win a major party's primary for the US House of Representatives.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Misty Snow wins a major-party US Senate primary
description: Misty Snow became the first openly transgender candidate to win a major party's primary for a seat in the US Senate.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: LGBT firsts at the 2016 Republican National Convention
description: At the Republican convention, Peter Thiel became the first speaker to state from the stage that he is gay, Rachel Hoff served as the first openly gay member of the party's platform committee, and nominee Donald Trump referenced the LGBTQ community in his 21 July acceptance speech — a first for a Republican nominee.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sarah McBride speaks at the Democratic National Convention
description: Activist Sarah McBride addressed the Democratic National Convention, becoming the first openly transgender person to speak at a major US party convention.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hillary Clinton engages LGBT media and marches at NYC Pride
description: Hillary Clinton wrote an op-ed for Philadelphia Gay News, the first major-party presidential candidate to write for an LGBT newspaper, and marched in the New York City Pride March as presumptive nominee.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Anderson Cooper moderates a US presidential debate
description: Anderson Cooper co-moderated the second debate between Hillary Clinton and Donald Trump alongside Martha Raddatz, becoming the first openly gay moderator of a US presidential debate.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kate Brown elected governor of Oregon
description: Kate Brown won the Oregon governorship, becoming the first openly bisexual person — and the first openly LGBT person — elected governor of a US state.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Indonesia's president speaks in defense of LGBT rights
description: President Joko Widodo stated publicly that he stood for protecting the rights of LGBT people in Indonesia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Katherine Zappone becomes Ireland's first openly lesbian minister
description: Katherine Zappone was appointed to the Irish cabinet, the country's first openly lesbian government minister.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Two openly gay candidates run for the Russian parliament
description: For the first time, two openly gay men stood as candidates in elections to Russia's parliament.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Malcolm Turnbull attends the Sydney Mardi Gras
description: Malcolm Turnbull became the first sitting Australian prime minister to attend the Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras; opposition leader Bill Shorten also attended.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Iceland's president takes part in Reykjavík Pride
description: President Guðni Th. Jóhannesson gave a speech at Reykjavík Pride 2016, becoming the first sitting president of any country to participate in a pride parade.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Justin Trudeau marches in a pride parade
description: Justin Trudeau became the first sitting Canadian prime minister to march in a pride parade.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Geraldine Roman elected to the Philippine Congress
description: Geraldine Roman won a seat in the House of Representatives, becoming the first openly transgender woman elected to Congress in the Philippines.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UN Security Council condemns the Orlando nightclub shooting
description: The UN Security Council condemned the attack on the Pulse nightclub in Orlando — the first Security Council statement to use language recognizing violence targeting the LGBT community.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Activists send a pride flag into the stratosphere
description: Using a high-altitude balloon, activists flew a rainbow pride flag about 21 miles (34 km) above Earth in August 2016 — the first pride flag carried into the stratosphere.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stonewall National Monument designated
description: On 24 June 2016 President Barack Obama designated the Stonewall National Monument, covering the Stonewall Inn, Christopher Street Park and the adjacent block — the first US national monument honoring an LGBT historic site.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Furies Collective house listed as a DC historic site
description: The row house on 11th Street SE that housed the Furies Collective was voted unanimously into the DC Inventory of Historic Sites, the city's first lesbian-related historic landmark.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Edificio Comunidad de Orgullo Gay joins the National Register
description: The Edificio Comunidad de Orgullo Gay in Puerto Rico became the first Latino LGBT site listed on the US National Register of Historic Places.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Navy ship named for Harvey Milk
description: The USNS Harvey Milk was formally named at a ceremony in San Francisco on 16 August 2016, the first US Navy vessel named for an openly gay leader; Milk had served as a Navy diving officer in the early 1950s.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mya Taylor wins an Independent Spirit Award
description: Mya Taylor won the Independent Spirit Award for Best Supporting Female, becoming the first openly transgender actor to win one of the awards.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Erin O'Flaherty crowned Miss Missouri
description: Erin O'Flaherty won the Miss Missouri title, making her the first openly lesbian contestant to compete for Miss America.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First endowed chair of transgender studies created
description: A two-million-dollar gift from Jennifer Pritzker's foundation endowed the world's first academic chair of transgender studies, at the University of Victoria in British Columbia, with Aaron Devor chosen as inaugural chair.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: United Nations creates an LGBT human rights watchdog
description: The United Nations voted to establish its first independent watchdog post monitoring human rights violations against LGBT people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
